cards.desc:
## 1. Technical Fundamentals

### Footwork

- Core principle: Dance on the balls of your feet
  - Better control over weight changes
  - Easier to maintain level movement without bounce
- Exceptions:
  - When taking large steps (makes pointing feet difficult)
  - For bouncy music styles (e.g., country dancing)

### Energy Management

- Match your partner's energy level - mismatches look bad in competitions
- When partner dances at lower energy (e.g., at 3/10):
  - Dance slightly higher (e.g., at 4/10) instead of jumping to 7/10
  - slowly try to raise their energy
  - Encourage through:
    - Nodding at their good moves
    - Giving space for expression
    - Building on their movements (e.g., copying their body wave and adding your style)
    - Leading playful movements f.e. [https://team.jive.berlin/f/44665](https://team.jive.berlin/f/44665 "‌")
- Managing high energy:
  - Think of it like driving - going faster doesn't always mean better
  - Ground yourself and redirect energy to planning:
    - "What move haven't I done yet?"
    - "What is my partner doing that I can build upon?"
  - Goal: Learn to recognize when partner is going too high and help ground them

### Dance Interpretation

- Listen to partner's interpretation before imposing yours
- Key questions to ask yourself:
  - Are they dancing flowy or elegant?
  - Which musical elements are they responding to?

## 2. Training Methods

### Deliberate Limitations

Practice with restrictions to develop other skills:

- Make 8-count pauses where you do nothing
- Dance blindfolded
- Limit your go-to moves
- Ask: "What skills can I develop when my usual tools aren't available?"

### Competition Preparation

- Energy management:
  - Keep first rounds simple and clean
  - Save complex moves for later rounds
  - Avoid unpolished moves entirely
- Mental check-in during doubts:
  - "Am I executing moves?"
  - "Am I on beat?"
  - "What other skills can shine right now?"

## 3. Musical Adaptation

### Challenging Music

When dancing to uninspiring songs:

- Focus on alternative skills:
  - Clean execution of moves
  - Basic techniques
  - Space utilization
- Let your partner shine if they're inspired
- It's okay to fake enthusiasm - focus on technical challenges instead

### Energy Generation

- Energy starts in the mind
- Practice self-motivation:
  - Even with "boring" songs, challenge yourself to go all out
  - Example: Transform "I don't want to dance to this" into "Let's get crazy with this"
  - Result: Often leads to unexpectedly fun dances

## 4. Practice Focus

### Move Selection

- Trust your existing repertoire
- Competition principle: A move done badly costs more than not doing it at all
- Regularly evaluate: Are specific moves consistently causing problems?

### Fundamental Training

- In beginner lessons, focus on:
  - Weight shifts
  - Hip movement
  - Step variations
- Experiment with footwork from other styles:
  - House dance patterns
  - Shuffle steps
  - Ask: When can these be integrated into basic moves?

### Freestyle Practice

- Approach as an active listener
- Focus on:
  - Picking up partner's style
  - Amplifying their choices
  - Building on their interpretation
cards.name: Dancefest 24 - Mevans - Ki

cards.desc:
# Communication

3 different possible leads

## Anchor Lead:

- Providing a high tension point of stability.
- generally not moving

## Dead Lead

- Signaling to Partner that they can play
- 0 Tension
- Tension needs to be instigated again to go back to lead and follow

### instigating Tension

- partner is playing
- move with them as if you led the movement to build connection again
- then lead new movement

## Play Lead

- Signaling that a play movement is coming
- Tense up and provide tension and visual clues

### going back to leading

- release the tension a bit backwards
- spring into the next movement
- Alternativly follows can build connection again when they are ready
  - 1 beat before the next movement should start

cards.desc:
# Tanztraining Struktur

## Gesamtkonzept

### Ein Thema pro Monat

Jeden Monat fokussiert ihr euch auf ein spezifisches Thema (z.B. Dynamics, Balance, Musicality, etc.)

**Beim Training:**

- Spezifisches Feedback zum aktuellen Monatsthema einholen
- Inspiration sammeln
- Im Freestyle testen und experimentieren
- Mehr Fokus durch **60s Practice**
  - Erst kurze Sequenzen üben
  - Ganze Lieder später für Ausdauertraining

---

## Dienstags-Training (2-wöchentlich)

**Dauer:** 1 Stunde
**Charakter:** Strukturiert und fokussiert

### Trainingsprinzipien

#### Viele Repetitionen

- Konzentration auf Wiederholungen für Muskelgedächtnis

#### Low-Impact-Versionen

Für anstrengende Elemente nur die aktuell relevanten Parts üben:

- Nur das Intro
- Nur 1 Spin statt mehrere
- Einzelne Sequenzen isolieren

#### Separate Übungen

- Balance halten
- Haltung
- Kraft

---

## Beispiel-Training: Thema "Dynamics"

### Aufbau (60 Minuten)

#### 1. Aufwärmen (5 min)

- Dynamic Stretching

#### 2. Timing-Übungen (5 min)

- Verschiedene Timings durchgehen:
  - Half-Time
  - Normal
  - Double-Time
- Übergänge zwischen den Timings üben
- Zählen hilft dabei sehr

#### 3. Moves & Sequenzen (20 min)

- Bekannte Moves lernen und üben
- **Vorbereitung:** Moves zum nächsten Training raussuchen
- Fokus auf das Monatsthema (hier: Dynamics)

#### 4. Konsolidierungspause (5 min)

- Kurze Pause zum Verarbeiten
- Mental nachvollziehen, was geübt wurde

#### 5. 60s Practice mit Video-Feedback (20 min)

- 60-Sekunden-Sequenzen tanzen
- Aufnehmen und gemeinsam analysieren
- Spezifisches Feedback zum Monatsthema

#### 6. Entwicklung & Kreativität (10 min)

- Brainstorming neuer Ideen
- Kreative Phase
- Neue Bewegungen entwickeln

#### 7. Cool-Down (5 min)

- Dehnen
- Entspannen
- Kurzes Feedback zur Session

---

## Notizen & To-Dos

**Für nächstes Training:**

- [ ] Moves und Sequenzen zum aktuellen Thema recherchieren
